what_it_does: Acceptance test for a vector store that keeps separate embeddings for the same source item under different projection spaces, using composed vec://...#space= addresses, explicit space/source fields, per-space k-NN, default-space isolation, per-space incremental indexing, persistence reload, and fail-loud validation.
observations: Uses deterministic embedding stubs so no live embed service is required.
Defines two projection geometries, principle and topic, over the same three source items.
Builds principle, topic, and default-space indexes in the same FsStore.
Checks the composed address grammar vec://code://x/alpha#space=principle and bare default address.
Verifies records carry explicit space and source fields.
Per-space k-NN returns source ids and produces different neighbor order in each space.
Default/unspaced queries and index_addresses see only bare entries, never spaced ones.
FsStore.ALL_SPACES enumerates all entries across spaces.
Incremental builds skip unchanged entries within a space but re-embed the same items for a new space.
Persistence reload preserves both spaced and unspaced entries.
put_vector with a spaced address but no source raises ValueError.
standouts: Cross-space distinguishability: the same item has different nearest neighbors in principle-space versus topic-space.
No-regression guard: unspaced/default queries are isolated from spaced entries to avoid dimension mismatch and corpus pollution.
Per-space incremental diff keys prevent the same source item from being falsely skipped across different spaces.
conventions: Module-level private constants and helpers prefixed with underscore.
Global PASS counter incremented by a local check helper.
tempfile directory created and removed in a try/finally block.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN and inserts project root before imports.
Extensive narrative docstring explains intent and acceptance criteria.
concerns: check mutates a global PASS counter.
_stub and _counting closures ignore transport, model, dim, and **kw, which could hide signature drift.
DEFAULT_CORPUS uses _stub({}) so all default vectors fall back to the same value, making default-space ranking less meaningful.
ALL_SPACES count assertion assumes exactly three spaces and three items.
The put_vector failure test catches generic ValueError rather than a specific exception/message.
notes: A hermetic acceptance test that locks down multi-space vector storage behavior and guards against default-space regression.
questions: What exactly are the C1 address grammar and C4 Resolver Protocol referenced in the docstring?
How do FsStore.space_address, get_vector, put_vector, index_addresses, and query_index implement the space/source handling?
What is the intended behavior when querying a space that has no entries?
How does the live embedding transport map to the stub function signature?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual tests: it exercises space-keyed storage, per-space retrieval, default-space isolation, incremental builds, persistence, and fail-loud validation.
apparent_themes: Multi-projection vector spaces for the same source item.
Explicit address composition and metadata fields.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish an acceptance contract for a vector store that supports multiple named projection spaces while remaining portable to a future Supabase-backed implementation.
novelty: Explicit per-space vector keys composed as vec://<source>#space=<space> with separate source and space record fields, and an acceptance test that proves the same item is cross-space distinguishable by neighbor geometry.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that defines expected behavior for multi-space vector storage, per-space retrieval, default-space isolation, incremental builds, persistence, and error handling.
summary_for_embedding: Hermetic acceptance test for space-keyed vector indexes that stores the same source item under multiple projection spaces and verifies per-space k-NN, default-space isolation, incremental diffing, and persistence.
intention_for_embedding: To lock down the contract that a source item can have distinct embeddings per projection space without polluting the default unspaced corpus.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: A simple assertion wrapper used throughout the file; it asserts a condition, increments the module-level PASS counter, and prints an ok message.

symbols.does: Factory returning a deterministic embedding function for a lookup table.
symbols.description: Builds a closure that maps input strings to pre-defined vectors from table, ignoring transport/model/dim parameters, used to simulate embedding services hermetically.
Returns the vector associated with each input string from the configured table, with a fallback [0.0,0.0,1.0]; ignores all other arguments so the test can inject known geometry.

symbols.does: Factory returning an embedding function that counts how many inputs it embeds.
symbols.description: Wraps a lookup table like _stub but increments calls['n'] by the batch size, enabling the test to verify incremental re-embedding behavior across spaces.
Looks up vectors from the configured table while recording the number of embedding requests, used to assert that unchanged spaces skip re-embedding and new spaces do not.
